2604.08819.title: SenBen: Sensitive Scene Graphs for Explainable Content Moderation
2604.08819.paper_summary: Content moderation systems classify images as safe or unsafe but lack spatial grounding and interpretability: they cannot explain what sensitive behavior was detected, who is involved, or where it occurs. We introduce the Sensitive Benchmark (SenBen), the first large-scale scene graph benchmark for sensitive content, comprising 13,999 frames from 157 movies annotated with Visual Genome-style scene graphs (25 object classes, 28 attributes including affective states such as pain, fear, aggression, and distress, 14 predicates) and 16 sensitivity tags across 5 categories. We distill a frontier VLM into a compact 241M student model using a multi-task recipe that addresses vocabulary imbalance in autoregressive scene graph generation through suffix-based object identity, Vocabulary-Aware Recall (VAR) Loss, and a decoupled Query2Label tag head with asymmetric loss, yielding a +6.4 percentage point improvement in SenBen Recall over standard cross-entropy training. On grounded scene graph metrics, our student model outperforms all evaluated VLMs except Gemini models and all commercial safety APIs, while achieving the highest object detection and captioning scores across all models, at $7.6\times$ faster inference and $16\times$ less GPU memory.
2604.08819.paper_summary_zh: 內容審核系統將圖像分類為安全或不安全，但缺乏空間基礎和可解釋性：它們無法解釋檢測到的敏感行為是什麼、涉及誰或發生在哪裡。<lb/>我們引入了敏感基準（Sensitive Benchmark，SenBen），這是首個針對敏感內容的大規模場景圖基準，包含來自157部電影的13,999幀，並用Visual Genome風格的場景圖進行標註（25個物體類別，28個屬性，包括疼痛、恐懼、侵略和痛苦等情感狀態，14個謂詞）以及5個類別的16個敏感性標籤。<lb/>我們將前沿的VLM提煉為一個緊湊的241M學生模型，使用多任務配方，通過基於後綴的物體身份、詞彙感知回憶（Vocabulary-Aware Recall，VAR）損失和具有不對稱損失的解耦Query2Label標籤頭來解決自回歸場景圖生成中的詞彙不平衡問題，從而在SenBen回憶上比標準交叉熵訓練提高了6.4個百分點。<lb/>在基於場景圖的度量上，我們的學生模型在所有評估的VLM中表現優於除Gemini模型外的所有模型以及所有商業安全API，同時在所有模型中實現了最高的物體檢測和標題生成分數，推理速度快了7.6倍，GPU內存使用量減少了16倍。
2604.08819.author: Fatih Cagatay Akyon et.al.
